-- $Id: testes/errors.lua $
-- See Copyright Notice in file all.lua

print("testing errors")

local debug = require"debug"

-- avoid problems with 'strict' module (which may generate other error messages)
local mt = getmetatable(_G) or {}
local oldmm = mt.__index
mt.__index = nil

local function checkerr (msg, f, ...)
  local st, err = pcall(f, ...)
  assert(not st and string.find(err, msg))
end


local function doit (s)
  local f, msg = load(s)
  if not f then return msg end
  local cond, msg = pcall(f)
  return (not cond) and msg
end


local function checkmessage (prog, msg, debug)
  local m = doit(prog)
  if debug then print(m, msg) end
  assert(string.find(m, msg, 1, true))
end

local function checksyntax (prog, extra, token, line)
  local msg = doit(prog)
  if not string.find(token, "^<%a") and not string.find(token, "^char%(")
    then token = "'"..token.."'" end
  token = string.gsub(token, "(%p)", "%%%1")
  local pt = string.format([[^%%[string ".*"%%]:%d: .- near %s$]],
                           line, token)
  assert(string.find(msg, pt))
  assert(string.find(msg, msg, 1, true))
end


-- test error message with no extra info
assert(doit("error('hi', 0)") == 'hi')

-- test error message with no info
assert(doit("error()") == nil)


-- test common errors/errors that crashed in the past
assert(doit("table.unpack({}, 1, n=2^30)"))
assert(doit("a=math.sin()"))
assert(not doit("tostring(1)") and doit("tostring()"))
assert(doit"tonumber()")
assert(doit"repeat until 1; a")
assert(doit"return;;")
assert(doit"assert(false)")
assert(doit"assert(nil)")

  print "testing memory error message"
  local a = {}
  for i = 1, 10000 do a[i] = true end   -- preallocate array
  collectgarbage()
  T.totalmem(T.totalmem() + 10000)
  -- force a memory error (by a small margin)
  local st, msg = pcall(function()

BASE="$(basename "$TEST_FILE" .lua)"
COMBINED="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.combined.lua"

run_with_timeout() {
    local src_file="$1"

    src="$(cat "$src_file")"
    if command -v gtimeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        gtimeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
    elif command -v timeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then

        wait "$pid" 2>/dev/null
        local rc=$?
        kill "$watcher" &>/dev/null
        wait "$watcher" 2>/dev/null || true
        return $rc
    fi

_soft = true
_port = true

local function doit (s)
  local f, msg = load(s)
  if not f then return msg end
  local cond, msg = pcall(f)
  return (not cond) and msg
end

local checks = {
  {"a = {} + 1", "arithmetic"},
  {"a = {} | 1", "bitwise operation"},
  {"a = {} < 1", "attempt to compare"},
  {"a = {} <= 1", "attempt to compare"},
  {"aaa=1; bbbb=2; aaa=math.sin(3)+bbbb(3)", "global 'bbbb'"},
  {"aaa={}; do local aaa=1 end aaa:bbbb(3)", "method 'bbbb'"},
  {"local a={}; a.bbbb(3)", "field 'bbbb'"},
  {"aaa={13}; local bbbb=1; aaa[bbbb](3)", "number"},
  {"aaa=(1)..{}", "a table value"},
  {"a = {_ENV = {}}; print(a._ENV.x + 1)", "field 'x'"},
}

-- Override checkmessage globally to log failures instead of stopping
local _cm_count = 0

local function doit (s)
  local f, msg = load(s)
  if not f then return msg end
  local cond, msg = pcall(f)
  return (not cond) and msg
end

local function my_checkmessage (prog, msg, debug)
  _cm_count = _cm_count + 1
  local m = doit(prog)
  if not (m and string.find(m, msg, 1, true)) then
    print("CHECKMESSAGE FAIL #"..(_cm_count).." prog="..tostring(prog).." expected_msg="..tostring(msg).." got_m="..tostring(m))
    return
  end
end

local function my_checksyntax(prog, extra, token, line)
  -- mimic check but don't fail
  local msg = doit(prog)
  if not msg then
    print("CHECKSYNTAX FAIL: doit returned nil for prog=" .. tostring(prog))
    return
  end
  if not string.find(token, "^<%a") and not string.find(token, "^char%(")
    then token = "'"..token.."'" end
  token = string.gsub(token, "(%p)", "%%%1")
  local pt = string.format([[^%%[string ".*"%%]:%d: .- near %s$]],
                           line, token)
  if not string.find(msg, pt) then
    print("CHECKSYNTAX FAIL: msg='" .. msg .. "' did not match pt='" .. pt .. "'")
  end
end

local function my_checkerr (msg, f, ...)
  local st, err = pcall(f, ...)
  if not (not st and string.find(err, msg)) then
    print("CHECKERR FAIL: msg pattern=" .. tostring(msg) .. " st=" .. tostring(st) .. " err=" .. tostring(err))
  end
end

-- Set up env to load errors.lua with overrides
_soft = true
_port = true
_nomsg = true
_U = false
arg = arg or {}
_G = _G or _ENV
if _VERSION == nil then _VERSION = "Lua 5.4" end

-- Inject our overrides into the loaded chunk's env
local env = setmetatable({
  checkmessage = my_checkmessage,
  checksyntax = my_checksyntax,
  checkerr = my_checkerr,
  print = print,
  doit = doit,
  -- pass through standard libraries
}, {__index = _G, __newindex = _G})

local chunk, lerr = loadfile("reference/lua-c/testes/errors.lua", "t")
if not chunk then print("LOAD ERR:", lerr); return end

-- Can't easily inject because errors.lua redefines them as local.
-- Instead, run normally and capture errors via xpcall.
local ok, err = xpcall(chunk, debug.traceback)
print("XPCALL ok=" .. tostring(ok))
if not ok then print("err=" .. tostring(err)) end

  local st, err = pcall(f, ...)

  local f, msg = load(s)

  local msg = doit(prog)
  if not string.find(token, "^<%a") and not string.find(token, "^char%(")

  token = string.gsub(token, "(%p)", "%%%1")
  local pt = string.format([[^%%[string ".*"%%]:%d: .- near %s$]],

local function checkerr (msg, f, ...)
  local st, err = pcall(f, ...)
  assert(not st and string.find(err, msg))
end


local function doit (s)
  local f, msg = load(s)
  if not f then return msg end
  local cond, msg = pcall(f)
  return (not cond) and msg
end


local function checkmessage (prog, msg, debug)
  local m = doit(prog)
  if debug then print(m, msg) end
  if not (m and string.find(m, msg, 1, true)) then io.stderr:write(string.format("CHECKMESSAGE FAIL prog=%s expected=%s got=%s\n", tostring(prog):sub(1,80), tostring(msg), tostring(m))) end
  assert(string.find(m, msg, 1, true))
end

local function checksyntax (prog, extra, token, line)
  local msg = doit(prog)
  if not string.find(token, "^<%a") and not string.find(token, "^char%(")
    then token = "'"..token.."'" end
  token = string.gsub(token, "(%p)", "%%%1")
  local pt = string.format([[^%%[string ".*"%%]:%d: .- near %s$]],
                           line, token)
  assert(string.find(msg, pt))
  assert(string.find(msg, msg, 1, true))
end
